bibliographic_data.title: Comprehensive Evaluation of ChatGPT Reliability Through Multilingual Inquiries
bibliographic_data.abstract: Ethical Consideration: Please be aware that this paper may contain potentially harmful or aggressive content. Such content is solely used for the analysis and testing of ChatGPT and does not imply any intent to promote criminal activities. ChatGPT is currently the most popular large language model (LLM), with over 100 million users, making a significant impact on people’s lives. However, due to the presence of jailbreak vulnerabilities, ChatGPT might have negative effects on people’s lives, potentially even facilitating criminal activities. Testing whether ChatGPT can cause jailbreak is crucial because it can enhance ChatGPT’s security, reliability, and social responsibility. Inspired by previous research revealing the varied performance of LLMs in different language translations, we suspected that wrapping prompts in multiple languages might lead to ChatGPT jailbreak. To investigate this, we designed a study with a fuzzing testing approach to analyzing ChatGPT's cross-linguistic proficiency. Our study includes three strategies by automatically posing different formats of malicious questions to ChatGPT: (1) each malicious question involving only one language, (2) multilingual malicious questions, (3) specifying that ChatGPT responds in a language different from the questions. In addition, we also combine our strategies by utilizing prompt injection templates to wrap the three aforementioned types of questions. We examined a total of 7,892 Q&A data points, discovering that multilingual wrapping can indeed lead to ChatGPT’s jailbreak, with different wrapping strategies having varying effects on jailbreak probability. Prompt injection can amplify the probability of jailbreak caused by multilingual wrapping. This study provides insights for OpenAI developers to enhance ChatGPT’s support for language diversity and inclusion.

core_content.novelty_claim: The study demonstrates that wrapping prompts in multiple languages can lead to ChatGPT jailbreak, with different wrapping strategies having varying effects on jailbreak probability.
core_content.key_methodology: A fuzzing testing approach was used to analyze ChatGPT's cross-linguistic proficiency. The study included three strategies: (1) each malicious question involving only one language, (2) multilingual malicious questions, and (3) specifying that ChatGPT responds in a language different from the questions. Prompt injection templates were also utilized.
outcomes: Multilingual wrapping can lead to ChatGPT jailbreak
Different wrapping strategies have varying effects on jailbreak probability
Prompt injection can amplify the probability of jailbreak caused by multilingual wrapping
core_content.full_text_markdown: ChatGPT is currently the most popular large language model (LLM) with over 100 million users. Due to its jailbreak vulnerabilities, it may have negative effects on people’s lives and potentially facilitate criminal activities. To enhance ChatGPT's security, reliability, and social responsibility, this study investigates whether wrapping prompts in multiple languages can lead to jailbreak. The study uses a fuzzing testing approach with three strategies: (1) each malicious question involving only one language, (2) multilingual malicious questions, and (3) specifying that ChatGPT responds in a different language from the questions. Prompt injection templates are also utilized. A total of 7,892 Q&A data points were examined, revealing that multilingual wrapping can indeed lead to jailbreak with varying effects on probability. This study provides insights for OpenAI developers to enhance ChatGPT's support for language diversity and inclusion.
